engagement.name: HubSpot CRM Implementation
engagement.scopeSource: Milestone Group HubSpot CRM Proposal - Signed.md

pricing.paymentTerms: 4 installments of $6,500 every 15 days, Net-15. 5% technology & administrative fee applied to each invoice.

phases.name: Contacts & Activity Capture

workstreams.name: CRM Architecture & Setup
tasks: Review current HubSpot portal access and document baseline state
Configure portal name, logo, favicon, timezone, currency, and date format
Define user role structure: Admin, Sales Manager, Sales Rep
Create user accounts for all 10 users across 4 offices (Atlanta, Dallas, Denver, South Florida)
Configure team structure: create teams by office location
Configure permission sets per role (contact visibility, deal access, report access)
Set up security settings: login notifications, 2FA requirements for admins
Establish naming conventions document for properties, lists, and workflows
QA: verify all 10 users can log in with correct role and data visibility

workstreams.name: Contact & Company Management
tasks: Run properties workshop with Jason Wise: map all required custom contact and company fields
Create custom contact properties (persona, broker type, relationship tier, acquisition focus, etc.)
Create custom company properties (entity type, market, PE firm vs. portfolio company, AUM, etc.)
Configure lifecycle stages for BD pipeline (prospect → active → closed)
Configure lead statuses for pipeline progression
Build parent-child company hierarchy (PE firm → portfolio company structure)
Set up association labels: broker, lender, investment partner, evangelist, acquisition target
Configure persona picklist options: broker, evangelist, investment partner, lender, competitor
Configure deduplication detection rules (match on email, phone, name+company)
Build active list: acquisition target contacts
Build active list: broker contacts by office/territory
QA: create 5 sample companies with parent-child relationships and verify associations

workstreams.name: Data Migration: Contact Import
tasks: Export templates sent to all Milestone team members (complete — sent Apr 10)
Receive Outlook contact exports from Jason, Robert, David, Rich, Jim (target: Apr 18, hard deadline: Apr 25)
Receive Zoho CRM export (contacts and associated notes only; other Zoho modules excluded)
Audit received data: record counts per source, field coverage gaps
Build field mapping matrix: source columns → HubSpot property IDs
Define duplicate handling rules with Jason Wise (owner priority, cross-office contact logic)
Define contact ownership assignment rules by office/territory
Clean and normalize data: standardize company names, phone formats, email validation
Deduplicate in staging: apply ownership rules, flag unresolvable conflicts for manual review
Execute test load: ~500 contacts
Review test load results with Jason Wise: duplicate rate, field mapping, ownership accuracy
Fix mapping or deduplication issues identified in test load
Execute full production import
Post-migration QA: verify record count, associations, and owner assignments
Deliver migration summary to Jason Wise (record counts, duplicate rate, sources)

workstreams.name: Email & Communication Setup
tasks: Obtain Microsoft 365 admin credentials from Milestone
Configure email tracking domain settings in HubSpot
Connect each user's Outlook inbox to HubSpot (10 users, 10 connections)
Configure email tracking settings per user (auto-log, open tracking, click tracking)
Set up email domain exclusion rules (internal @milestonegroup.com, personal domains)
Configure auto-association rules (link emails to contacts and deals automatically)
QA: send test emails, verify activity logs appear on contact records
Document user-by-user connection status and any per-user configuration issues

workstreams.name: Integration: Outlook Calendar & Contact Sync
workstreams.note: Signed proposal phases this in Phase 3, but Phase 1 success criteria require calendar sync working. Keeping in Phase 1; can shift to Phase 3 if timeline is constrained.
tasks: Configure Outlook Calendar sync in HubSpot (App Marketplace → Microsoft Calendar)
Set sync permissions: define which calendar events log to CRM (external meetings only vs. all)
Configure meeting auto-log rules: attendee matching to HubSpot contacts
Set up Outlook contact sync rules (HubSpot wins on conflict)
QA: create test meeting, verify it appears on contact timeline
Complete per-user calendar sync validation across all 10 users
Document known limitations: sync latency, contact match rules, known edge cases

workstreams.name: Automations & Workflows
tasks: Workflow requirements session with Jason Wise: confirm all 8 automation use cases
WF1: Auto-log activity — trigger email/meeting logs on contact records
WF2: Auto-contact creation — create contact from new Outlook email (with domain exclusions)
WF3: Follow-up reminder — 30-day no-activity task trigger
WF4: Domain exclusion filter — suppress @milestonegroup.com and personal email interactions
WF5: Lead routing — assign owner by office/territory on contact creation
WF6: Deal stage notification — notify owner when deal advances to a new stage
WF7–WF8: TBD — confirm with Jason Wise in working session
QA: test each workflow with sample data (triggers, actions, enrollment criteria)
Fix bugs identified in workflow testing
Document each workflow: trigger logic, actions, and admin contact for changes

successCriteria: All 10 users can log in with correct roles and permissions
Parent-child company hierarchy reflects Milestone portfolio structure
Contacts migrated from all sources with <5% duplicate rate
Email and calendar sync working for all users
Auto-contact creation firing on new email/meeting interactions
Task reminders and follow-up workflows triggering correctly

phases.name: Deal Pipeline & Reporting

workstreams.name: Pipeline & Deal Management
tasks: Pipeline design session with Jason Wise: confirm deal stages, required fields, and qualification criteria
Create deal pipeline with confirmed stage names (4–6 stages)
Configure required fields per stage (stage gate enforcement)
Create custom deal properties (property type, investment thesis, acquisition target, market, etc.)
Configure deal owner assignment rules
Set up deal views and filters by office/team
Configure multi-party associations: broker, lender, owner, and acquisition target linked to deals
Set up auto-link rules: associate contacts by role when added to a deal
Configure deal close date requirements and stage progression rules
QA: run 3 sample deals through pipeline, verify stage gates enforce required fields
QA: verify multi-party associations work correctly on sample deals
Fix any pipeline configuration issues identified in QA

workstreams.name: Reporting & Dashboards
tasks: Reporting requirements session with Jason Wise: confirm dashboard priorities and key metrics
BD Activity Dashboard — Report: activity by rep (calls, meetings, emails this week/month)
BD Activity Dashboard — Report: activity by office/territory
BD Activity Dashboard — Report: top reps by contact touches
Contact Engagement Dashboard — Report: lifecycle stage distribution
Contact Engagement Dashboard — Report: contacts with no activity in 30+ days
Contact Engagement Dashboard — Report: contacts created by source (migration vs. organic)
Deal Pipeline Dashboard — Report: pipeline by stage (count)
Deal Pipeline Dashboard — Report: deal age by stage
Deal Pipeline Dashboard — Report: new deals this month by owner
Configure scheduled report email delivery to Jason Wise (weekly digest)
Review all dashboards with Jason Wise: confirm metrics are reading correctly
Adjust reports based on feedback
Document report definitions and how to read each metric

successCriteria: Deal pipeline configured with stage gates enforcing required fields
Multi-party associations working (broker + lender + owner linked to deals)
Executive dashboard showing pipeline status, BD activity, and contact metrics
Sample deals flow through pipeline correctly with all associations intact

phases.name: Integrations, Training & Go-Live

workstreams.name: Integration: Fireflies
tasks: Confirm Fireflies subscription status and API access with Jason Wise (client action — required before configuration)
Connect Fireflies to HubSpot: authenticate in Fireflies dashboard
Configure auto-logging rules: define which meetings log to CRM
Configure contact matching logic: match meeting participants to HubSpot records by email
Set up activity note creation: transcript summary auto-created on contact record
Configure meeting type tagging (BD vs. internal, deal-related vs. relationship-building)
QA: run 2–3 test meetings, verify transcripts appear on correct contact records
Validate contact matching edge cases (new contacts, partial email matches)
Document known limitations and troubleshooting steps for admin

workstreams.name: Training: Admin
tasks: Identify 1–2 admin champions at Milestone
Build admin training agenda and materials
Training Session 1 (90 min): portal settings, user management, properties, lists
Training Session 2 (90 min): workflows, reports, troubleshooting common issues
Admin Q&A and sign-off: confirm admins can perform key tasks independently

workstreams.name: Training: End Users
tasks: Build end-user training materials (designed for varying tech comfort, ages 25–65)
Training Session 1 (60 min): daily CRM usage — contacts, activity feed, task management
Training Session 2 (60 min): deal pipeline — creating deals, advancing stages, associating contacts
Training Session 3 (45 min): mobile app setup and on-the-go workflows
Record all sessions for async reference and future new-hire onboarding
Collect training feedback from attendees

workstreams.name: UAT & Go-Live
tasks: Create UAT test scripts covering all configured workstreams (CRM-01 through INT-01)
Share test scripts with Jason Wise and Robert Strong before UAT session
UAT Session 1: power users (Jason, Robert) — walk through test scripts, log issues
Triage bugs from Session 1: classify as critical / high / low
Fix critical and high-priority bugs from Session 1
UAT Session 2: full team (all offices) — role-specific flows and general walkthrough
Fix remaining critical bugs from Session 2
Kyle Harbuck sign-off: UAT complete
Jason Wise sign-off: UAT complete
Execute go-live: confirm all users active, send go-live announcement to Milestone team
Hypercare Week 1: daily monitoring, user questions, fix adoption blockers
Hypercare Week 2: monitor usage, close out remaining open items

workstreams.name: Documentation & SOPs
tasks: Create admin reference guide (settings, user management, workflow editing, troubleshooting)
Create end-user daily workflow guide (logging activity, managing contacts, advancing deals, tasks)
Create mobile quick-start guide (app download, setup, key mobile actions)
Create CRM hygiene guide: weekly review process (contact cleanup, deal updates, task completion)
Deliver all documentation to Jason Wise in shared Google Drive data room

successCriteria: Fireflies integration auto-logging transcripts to correct contact records
All admins trained and able to manage properties, workflows, and reports independently
All end users trained across 4 offices including mobile app setup
UAT complete with all critical bugs resolved
Go-live executed with all users active
Documentation delivered: admin guide, end-user guide, mobile quick-start guide

crossPhaseWorkstreams.name: Project Management
crossPhaseWorkstreams.cadence: Weekly standups Monday 2:00 PM Central; phase reviews at end of each phase

risks.name: Data Quality Risk
risks.impact: Contacts scattered across Excel, OneNote, and email may contain duplicates and inconsistent formatting, increasing migration effort
risks.mitigation: Conduct data audit before import; deduplicate in staging area; small test loads before full migration; set up HubSpot duplicate detection

risks.name: Adoption Risk
risks.impact: Team members with varying tech comfort (ages 25-65) may resist CRM adoption, reverting to Excel/OneNote habits
risks.mitigation: Start with minimal required fields; automate capture; role-specific training; leadership champions usage; phased rollout builds confidence

risks.name: Scope Creep
risks.impact: Client may request Asana integration, advanced reporting, or deal scoring before Phase 1 is stable
risks.mitigation: Enforce phased approach; document Phase 2/3 features as out of scope for current phase; change order process for additions

risks.name: Stakeholder Availability
risks.impact: Key decision-makers distributed across 4 offices with active deal flow, making scheduling difficult
risks.mitigation: Recurring weekly check-ins; async communication for approvals; one primary POC (Jason Wise) for decisions

risks.name: Data Migration Volume
risks.impact: Contact volume described as thousands without exact count; actual volume could increase migration hours
risks.mitigation: Request full contact export by week 2; assess volume before finalizing migration plan; cap initial import if needed

risks.name: Integration Complexity: Fireflies
risks.impact: Fireflies integration may have limitations on transcript format, contact matching, or API rate limits
risks.mitigation: Test integration with sample meetings before go-live; validate contact matching logic; document limitations

risks.name: Licensing Cost Uncertainty
risks.impact: HubSpot tier selection may need adjustment; features assumed in starter may require upgrade
risks.mitigation: Validate feature requirements against tier matrix before purchase; negotiate during HubSpot sales quarter

risks.name: Timeline Risk
risks.impact: Dependencies between contact migration and automation setup could create bottlenecks
risks.mitigation: Sequence data migration before automation config; buffer between phases; avoid parallel dependent workstreams

assumptions: HubSpot Sales Hub Starter or Smart CRM tier (final tier TBD after scoping call)
Up to 10 licensed CRM users across 4 offices
Microsoft 365 (Outlook + Exchange) as primary email/calendar platform
2-3 reporting dashboards with up to 10 custom reports
Zoho migration includes contacts and associated notes only; other Zoho modules excluded
1 test data load before final migration
Fireflies.ai integration confirmed in signed proposal; client to provide API access before Phase 3
Phased rollout: contacts/activity first, deals second, integrations and training third
exclusions: Salesforce, Zoho, or any CRM other than HubSpot
Advanced deal flow management beyond basic pipeline (deal scoring, weighted pipeline)
Marketing automation (email campaigns, sequences, landing pages)
HubSpot Service Hub or Operations Hub features
Hardware procurement or IT infrastructure changes
Historical activity or deal data migration (contacts only)
clientResponsibilities: Provide complete contact exports from Excel, OneNote, and email by Apr 18 (hard deadline Apr 25)
Primary point of contact for decisions: Jason Wise (confirmed at kickoff)
Ensure stakeholder availability for discovery, UAT, and training sessions
Procure HubSpot licenses before implementation begins (confirmed at kickoff: licenses secured)
Procure or confirm Fireflies.ai subscription with API access before Phase 3
Provide Microsoft 365 admin access for Outlook email/calendar integration setup
Champion CRM adoption internally — leadership must set expectations for usage
Complete UAT testing within agreed timeline
Communicate deal stages and qualification criteria during pipeline design session (Phase 2)
